channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What to do if you lose your phone
item.description: Phones hold so much of our digital lives—emails, social media and bank accounts, photos, chat messages and more—that if they ever get stolen or go missing, it can cause major disruption beyond just the loss of a device.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Peacemaker season 2: HBO Max release date prediction, confirmed cast, possible plot, and more news and rumors
item.description: Here's everything we know about Peacemaker season 2, the DCU Chapter One series that's set to arrive sometime in 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: ICYMI: the week's 8 biggest tech news stories from Nintendo's new alarm clock, to Toyota's revolutionary EV charging tech
item.description: ICYMI here's the biggest tech news stories from Nintendo, Toyota, Panasonic, Google Gemini and more for October 12, 2024.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: Who gets to own ‘America’ online?
item.description: The handle @America is a valuable account name on any social media network—and in many cases, it’s also become a right-wing calling card. As the presidential election looms, America and its imagery have become increasingly partisan.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Robinhood cofounder Baiju Bhatt wants to create the Starlink of solar power
item.description: It’s been seven months since Robinhood cofounder Baiju Bhatt entered stealth mode. Last March, he left his CCO perch at the online brokerage valued at $23 billion to quietly shift gears.
item.dc:creator: Susan Karlin, Fast Company

item.title: AMD boosts AI performance in new line of enterprise PC chips
item.description: AMD’s big Advancing AI event in San Francisco on Thursday underlined how quickly the microprocessor industry has pivoted to artificial intelligence (AI) as its main sales pitch.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Key supplier of this critical semiconductor material is back in operation post-Helene
item.description: One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Pentagon releases final CMMC rule, paving way for implementation
item.description: The Pentagon has taken another big step forward to make CMMC a reality. DoD projects the requirements will start showing up in contracts by mid-2025. The post Pentagon releases final CMMC rule, paving way for implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Senators press Commerce Department to boost transparency about CHIPS Act contracts
item.description: A group of senators are urging the Commerce Department to be more transparent regarding the terms of agreements made under the CHIPS and Science Act, arguing the agency must ensure the U.S. semiconductor industry is “safe and sustainable.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Ben Horowitz’s dual support for Trump and Harris says a lot about the Valley’s AI politics
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. I’m Mark Sullivan, a senior writer at Fast Company, covering emerging tech, AI, and tech policy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One Tech Tip: Here's what you need to do before and after your phone is stolen or lost
item.description: Phones hold so much of our digital lives that if they ever get stolen or go missing, it can cause major disruption beyond just the loss of a device.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Exclusive: Headspace’s new AI chatbot will offer mental health support to subscribers
item.description: Mental health app Headspace has launched a generative AI-powered chatbot called Ebb for users to get instant, personalized support through life’s ebbs and flows, the company exclusively told Fast Company.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft unveils health care AI tools
item.description: Microsoft unveiled several new artificial intelligence (AI) tools on Thursday aiming to support health care organizations through medical imaging models, health care agent services and an AI-driven workflow solution for nurses.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Scully, The Hill

item.title: If your agency is buying artificial intelligence, what are you exactly buying anyhow?
item.description: "General intellectual property law in the United States is still trying to figure out AI and a number of features of AI," procurement attorney Dan Ramish said. The post If your agency is buying artificial intelligence, what are you exactly buying anyhow? first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Energy leader Petrobras sails toward sustainability with new supply chain solution
item.description: Brazilian oil, natural gas, and energy corporation Petrobras’ longtime mission has been to be the best in its field in terms of value creation with an emphasis on environmental responsibility.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Five days a week in the office? Forget it!
item.description: A few weeks ago, Amazon CEO Andy Jassy announced that all corporate employees, including AWS staffers, will be expected to work in the office five days a week beginning in January. I don’t work for Amazon, but if I did, I’d quit. Period. End of statement.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: When is the right time to dump an AI project?
item.description: Many organizations running AI pilots will inevitably encounter a key IT dilemma: when to pull the plug and move on.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Here’s what’s new in Office 2024
item.description: Microsoft has just released Office 2024, the new non-subscription version of its office suite. It’s available in three editions: Office Home 2024 for consumers, Office Home & Business 2024 for consumers and small businesses, and Office LTSC 2024 for midsize and large businesses.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta expands AI assistant to more countries and languages amid intensifying AI battle
item.description: Meta announced plans to roll out its AI-powered chatbot, Meta AI, in 21 new markets, intensifying its competition with OpenAI’s ChatGPT and Google’s Gemini.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity
item.description: Various industries are still dealing with the consequences of the July 2024 technology outage which led to a "blue screen of death" and extreme disruptions. The post Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: IoT-driven endpoint proliferation requires secure-by-design principles
item.description: The government needs to push the market in the direction of secure-by-design, including manufacturing, fielding and management of these new endpoints. The post IoT-driven endpoint proliferation requires secure-by-design principles first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Aryaka brings CASB into unified SASE fold
item.description: Aryaka continues to build out its SASE platform, adding new capabilities aimed at enhancing security and simplifying the adoption process for enterprises that want to consolidate their network and security deployments. At the heart of this update is Aryaka’s new cloud access security broker (CASB).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DoD wringing out zero trust concepts under assessment process
item.description: The Pentagon is also working with major cloud service providers like Microsoft and Google to evaluate their zero trust implementations. The post DoD wringing out zero trust concepts under assessment process first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Wi-Fi HaLow: Hands on with AsiaRF’s IoT network gateway
item.description: Wi-Fi HaLow (802.11ah) is a relatively new standard that provides extended range and penetration through walls and other obstacles, but the tradeoff is low throughput. HaLow’s sweet spot is industrial IoT, building automation, security, access control, utility smart meters and intelligent grids.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule said Sunday they have suspended the process of taking applications to use it — which numbered over 370 last month — as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, PIX 11
